title: oberna behen subsp. carpatica
intro: Oberna behen subsp. carpatica, also known as Alpine Bugloss or Carpenter's Bugloss, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the borage family (Boraginaceae). It is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe, with medicinal properties attributed to its leaves and roots, which contain alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and respiratory infections, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. Oberna behen subsp. carpatica can be used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally to harness its health benefits.
